mission.title: coredns: CoreDNS pods panic with no probe failures
mission.description: CoreDNS pods panic with no probe failures. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify coredns troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your coredns deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review coredns configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant coredns configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get configmap -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=coredns
```
**What happened**:

Recently, we have seen CoreDNS pods panic with no probe failures.  The pod logs show a panic and error yet there are no liveness nor readiness probe failures.

steps.title: Apply the fix for CoreDNS pods panic with no probe failures
steps.description:
On the surface, it looks like a bug upstream in k8s.io/client-go.

```
W0416 1848.103956       1 reflector.go:326] pkg/mod/k8s.io/client-go.17.2/tools/cache/reflector.go watch of *v1.Endpoints ended with: too old resource version: 3565458375 (3565460029)
```

I'm guessing from the message above,
```yaml
livenessProbe:
      failureThreshold: 5
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8080
        scheme: HTTP
      initialDelaySeconds: 60
      periodSeconds: 10
      successThreshold: 1
      timeoutSeconds: 5
```

steps.title: Upgrade coredns to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade coredns:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade coredns coredns/coredns --namespace coredns
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns
helm list -n coredns
```

steps.title: Confirm CoreDNS pods panic with no probe failures is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n coredns --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
On the surface, it looks like a bug upstream in k8s.io/client-go.

```
W0416 1848.103956       1 reflector.go:326] pkg/mod/k8s.io/client-go.17.2/tools/cache/reflector.go watch of *v1.Endpoints ended with: too old resource version: 3565458375 (3565460029)
```

I'm guessing from the message above, that the watch dies, and is not restarted.
codeSnippets:
livenessProbe:
      failureThreshold: 5
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8080
        scheme: HTTP
      initialDelaySeconds: 60
      periodSeconds: 10
      successThreshold: 1
      timeoutSeconds: 5

readinessProbe:
      failureThreshold: 3
      httpGet:
        path: /ready
        port: 8181
        scheme: HTTP
      periodSeconds: 10
      successThreshold: 1
      timeoutSeconds: 1

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with coredns installed or the issue environment reproducible.
